		direction = -direction
		state = State.REBOUNDING

func _flying_up(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED

func _flying_down(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _jumping(delta: float) -> void:
	# Apply gravity; cap descent at terminal so the jump arc matches falls.
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return

	# Mid-jump arrow input cancels the arc and starts a directional launch.
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		velocity = Vector2(-flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		velocity = Vector2(flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_down"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_DOWN
		return

	# No directional input — natural arc.
	if is_on_ceiling() and velocity.y < 0:
		velocity.y = 0.0
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED
	elif is_on_floor() and velocity.y >= 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _rebounding(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	var done := false
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Hit another wall before completing the 1-tile rebound — stop here.
		done = true
	elif (direction == 1 and position.x >= rebound_target_x) or \
		 (direction == -1 and position.x <= rebound_target_x):
		position.x = rebound_target_x
		done = true
	if done:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED

func _paused(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		state = State.FALLING

func _falling(delta: float) -> void:
	velocity.x = 0.0
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if is_on_floor():
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _dead(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		position = spawn_position
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		direction = 0
		state = State.IDLE

func _hit_hazard() -> bool:
	for i in get_slide_collision_count():
		var col := get_slide_collision(i)
		var other := col.get_collider()
		if other != null and other.has_meta("is_hazard"):
			_die()
			return true
	return false

func _die() -> void:
	velocity = Vector2.ZERO
	pause_timer = death_pause
	state = State.DEAD

class_name Player
extends CharacterBody2D

# Tile-based launch-and-stop player for Ricochet.
# State machine, no smooth easing — constant flight speed, gravity for falls
# and the Space jump arc. See games/ricochet/design/design.md for the spec.

const TILE_SIZE := 48.0

# Tuning — exposed for tweaking in the Godot Inspector once Player becomes a
# scene. Speeds/distances are in tiles (multiplied by TILE_SIZE in _ready).
@export_group("Tuning")
@export var flight_speed_tiles: float = 25.0       # all directed launches
@export var gravity_tiles: float = 10.0            # tiles / sec²
@export var terminal_velocity_tiles: float = 5.0   # fall speed cap
@export var jump_height_tiles: float = 2.0         # Space-jump peak above floor
@export var rebound_distance_tiles: float = 1.0
@export var pause_time: float = 0.1                # seconds at apex / after rebound, before falling
@export var death_pause: float = 0.5               # seconds dead before respawn

# Cached pixel-space values derived from the exports above (set in _ready).
var flight_speed: float
var gravity: float
var terminal_velocity: float
var jump_height: float
var rebound_distance: float
# Initial up-velocity to reach jump_height under gravity: v = sqrt(2*g*h).
var jump_initial_velocity: float

enum State {
	IDLE,           # standing on a floor, accepting input
	RISING,         # rising 1 tile vertically before a horizontal launch
	FLYING_H,       # cruising left or right at flight speed
	FLYING_UP,      # cruising up at flight speed
	FLYING_DOWN,    # cruising down at flight speed (only triggered mid-jump)
	JUMPING,        # vertical jump arc; input accepted during ascent AND descent
	REBOUNDING,     # 1-tile horizontal rebound after hitting a wall
	PAUSED,         # brief delay before falling under gravity
	FALLING,        # gravity-driven free fall, no input
	FALLING_INPUT,  # gravity-driven fall after a fly-up ceiling bump; input accepted
	DEAD,           # waiting to respawn
}

var state: State = State.IDLE
var direction: int = 0           # -1 left / +1 right while horizontal-launched
var rise_target_y: float = 0.0   # y-coord to stop the pre-launch rise at
var rebound_target_x: float = 0.0
var pause_timer: float = 0.0
var post_pause_state: State = State.FALLING  # state to enter when PAUSED ends
var spawn_position: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO

const TUNING_PATH := "res://config/player_tuning.json"

func _ready() -> void:
	_load_tuning(TUNING_PATH)
	flight_speed = flight_speed_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	gravity = gravity_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	terminal_velocity = terminal_velocity_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	jump_height = jump_height_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	rebound_distance = rebound_distance_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	jump_initial_velocity = sqrt(2.0 * gravity * jump_height)

	# Register the Space jump action if the project doesn't already define it.
	if not InputMap.has_action("jump"):
		InputMap.add_action("jump")
		var ev := InputEventKey.new()
		ev.physical_keycode = KEY_SPACE
		InputMap.action_add_event("jump", ev)

# Override @export defaults with values from a JSON file. Any keys present in
# the JSON win; missing keys / missing file / parse errors fall back to defaults.
func _load_tuning(path: String) -> void:
	if not FileAccess.file_exists(path):
		return
	var f := FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.READ)
	if f == null:
		push_warning("[player] could not open tuning file: %s" % path)
		return
	var data: Variant = JSON.parse_string(f.get_as_text())
	if typeof(data) != TYPE_DICTIONARY:
		push_warning("[player] tuning json is not an object: %s" % path)
		return
	for key in data:
		if key in self:
			set(key, data[key])
		else:
			push_warning("[player] unknown tuning key '%s' in %s" % [key, path])

func _physics_process(delta: float) -> void:
	match state:
		State.IDLE:        _idle(delta)
		State.RISING:      _rising(delta)
		State.FLYING_H:    _flying_h(delta)
		State.FLYING_UP:   _flying_up(delta)
		State.FLYING_DOWN: _flying_down(delta)
		State.JUMPING:     _jumping(delta)
		State.REBOUNDING:  _rebounding(delta)
		State.PAUSED:        _paused(delta)
		State.FALLING:       _falling(delta)
		State.FALLING_INPUT: _falling_input(delta)
		State.DEAD:          _dead(delta)

func _idle(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2.ZERO
	move_and_slide()
	if not is_on_floor():
		# Floor disappeared (e.g., destructible later) — fall.
		state = State.FALLING
		return

	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		rise_target_y = position.y - TILE_SIZE
		state = State.RISING
	elif Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		rise_target_y = position.y - TILE_SIZE
		state = State.RISING
	elif Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
	elif Input.is_action_just_pressed("jump"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -jump_initial_velocity)
		state = State.JUMPING
	# Down on floor: intentionally a no-op in v1.

func _rising(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Ceiling clipped the rise. Start horizontal flight from current y.
		state = State.FLYING_H
	elif position.y <= rise_target_y:
		position.y = rise_target_y  # snap to exact tile boundary
		state = State.FLYING_H

func _flying_h(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		rebound_target_x = position.x - direction * rebound_distance
		direction = -direction
		state = State.REBOUNDING

func _flying_up(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		post_pause_state = State.FALLING_INPUT
		state = State.PAUSED

func _flying_down(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _jumping(delta: float) -> void:
	# Apply gravity; cap descent at terminal so the jump arc matches falls.
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return

	# Mid-jump arrow input cancels the arc and starts a directional launch.
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		velocity = Vector2(-flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		velocity = Vector2(flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_down"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_DOWN
		return

	# No directional input — natural arc.
	if is_on_ceiling() and velocity.y < 0:
		velocity.y = 0.0
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED
	elif is_on_floor() and velocity.y >= 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _rebounding(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	var done := false
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Hit another wall before completing the 1-tile rebound — stop here.
		done = true
	elif (direction == 1 and position.x >= rebound_target_x) or \
		 (direction == -1 and position.x <= rebound_target_x):
		position.x = rebound_target_x
		done = true
	if done:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED

func _paused(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		state = State.FALLING

func _falling(delta: float) -> void:
	velocity.x = 0.0
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if is_on_floor():
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _dead(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		position = spawn_position
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		direction = 0
		state = State.IDLE

func _hit_hazard() -> bool:
	for i in get_slide_collision_count():
		var col := get_slide_collision(i)
		var other := col.get_collider()
		if other != null and other.has_meta("is_hazard"):
			_die()
			return true
	return false

func _die() -> void:
	velocity = Vector2.ZERO
	pause_timer = death_pause
	state = State.DEAD

		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _jumping(delta: float) -> void:
	# Apply gravity; cap descent at terminal so the jump arc matches falls.
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return

	# Mid-jump arrow input cancels the arc and starts a directional launch.
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		velocity = Vector2(-flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		velocity = Vector2(flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_down"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_DOWN
		return

	# No directional input — natural arc.
	if is_on_ceiling() and velocity.y < 0:
		velocity.y = 0.0
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED
	elif is_on_floor() and velocity.y >= 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _rebounding(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	var done := false
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Hit another wall before completing the 1-tile rebound — stop here.
		done = true
	elif (direction == 1 and position.x >= rebound_target_x) or \
		 (direction == -1 and position.x <= rebound_target_x):
		position.x = rebound_target_x
		done = true
	if done:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED

func _paused(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		state = post_pause_state
		post_pause_state = State.FALLING  # reset so unset transitions default to plain falling

func _falling(delta: float) -> void:
	velocity.x = 0.0
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if is_on_floor():
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _dead(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		position = spawn_position
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		direction = 0
		state = State.IDLE

func _hit_hazard() -> bool:
	for i in get_slide_collision_count():
		var col := get_slide_collision(i)
		var other := col.get_collider()
		if other != null and other.has_meta("is_hazard"):
			_die()
			return true
	return false

func _die() -> void:
	velocity = Vector2.ZERO
	pause_timer = death_pause
	state = State.DEAD

content.text:
Done. Summary of behavior:

- Press **Up** on floor → fly up. Hit ceiling → brief pause → enter **FALLING_INPUT** (gravity pulls you down, arrows are live).
- During this fall: **Left/Right** launches horizontal, **Up** launches up again (lets you ricochet vertically), **Down** snaps fall speed to `terminal_velocity` instantly. Land on floor → IDLE.
- Wall rebounds and Space-jump-into-ceiling still go to plain `FALLING` (input-locked, per the existing design spec).

Two scope notes — let me know if you want either changed:
1. Space-jump arc that rams the ceiling still falls input-locked. Want that to also use `FALLING_INPUT`?
2. The down-snap currently works once and gravity continues from there (terminal cap holds it). Holding down doesn't do anything extra. Sufficient, or want it tappable repeatedly?

class_name Player
extends CharacterBody2D

# Tile-based launch-and-stop player for Ricochet.
# State machine, no smooth easing — constant flight speed, gravity for falls
# and the Space jump arc. See games/ricochet/design/design.md for the spec.

const TILE_SIZE := 48.0

# Tuning — exposed for tweaking in the Godot Inspector once Player becomes a
# scene. Speeds/distances are in tiles (multiplied by TILE_SIZE in _ready).
@export_group("Tuning")
@export var flight_speed_tiles: float = 25.0       # all directed launches
@export var gravity_tiles: float = 10.0            # tiles / sec²
@export var terminal_velocity_tiles: float = 5.0   # fall speed cap
@export var jump_height_tiles: float = 2.0         # Space-jump peak above floor
@export var rebound_distance_tiles: float = 1.0
@export var pause_time: float = 0.1                # seconds at apex / after rebound, before falling
@export var death_pause: float = 0.5               # seconds dead before respawn

# Cached pixel-space values derived from the exports above (set in _ready).
var flight_speed: float
var gravity: float
var terminal_velocity: float
var jump_height: float
var rebound_distance: float
# Initial up-velocity to reach jump_height under gravity: v = sqrt(2*g*h).
var jump_initial_velocity: float

enum State {
	IDLE,           # standing on a floor, accepting input
	RISING,         # rising 1 tile vertically before a horizontal launch
	FLYING_H,       # cruising left or right at flight speed
	FLYING_UP,      # cruising up at flight speed
	FLYING_DOWN,    # cruising down at flight speed (only triggered mid-jump)
	JUMPING,        # vertical jump arc; input accepted during ascent AND descent
	REBOUNDING,     # 1-tile horizontal rebound after hitting a wall
	PAUSED,         # brief delay before falling under gravity
	FALLING,        # gravity-driven free fall, no input
	FALLING_INPUT,  # gravity-driven fall after a fly-up ceiling bump; input accepted
	DEAD,           # waiting to respawn
}

var state: State = State.IDLE
var direction: int = 0           # -1 left / +1 right while horizontal-launched
var rise_target_y: float = 0.0   # y-coord to stop the pre-launch rise at
var rebound_target_x: float = 0.0
var pause_timer: float = 0.0
var post_pause_state: State = State.FALLING  # state to enter when PAUSED ends
var spawn_position: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO

const TUNING_PATH := "res://config/player_tuning.json"

func _ready() -> void:
	_load_tuning(TUNING_PATH)
	flight_speed = flight_speed_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	gravity = gravity_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	terminal_velocity = terminal_velocity_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	jump_height = jump_height_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	rebound_distance = rebound_distance_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	jump_initial_velocity = sqrt(2.0 * gravity * jump_height)

	# Register the Space jump action if the project doesn't already define it.
	if not InputMap.has_action("jump"):
		InputMap.add_action("jump")
		var ev := InputEventKey.new()
		ev.physical_keycode = KEY_SPACE
		InputMap.action_add_event("jump", ev)

# Override @export defaults with values from a JSON file. Any keys present in
# the JSON win; missing keys / missing file / parse errors fall back to defaults.
func _load_tuning(path: String) -> void:
	if not FileAccess.file_exists(path):
		return
	var f := FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.READ)
	if f == null:
		push_warning("[player] could not open tuning file: %s" % path)
		return
	var data: Variant = JSON.parse_string(f.get_as_text())
	if typeof(data) != TYPE_DICTIONARY:
		push_warning("[player] tuning json is not an object: %s" % path)
		return
	for key in data:
		if key in self:
			set(key, data[key])
		else:
			push_warning("[player] unknown tuning key '%s' in %s" % [key, path])

func _physics_process(delta: float) -> void:
	match state:
		State.IDLE:        _idle(delta)
		State.RISING:      _rising(delta)
		State.FLYING_H:    _flying_h(delta)
		State.FLYING_UP:   _flying_up(delta)
		State.FLYING_DOWN: _flying_down(delta)
		State.JUMPING:     _jumping(delta)
		State.REBOUNDING:  _rebounding(delta)
		State.PAUSED:        _paused(delta)
		State.FALLING:       _falling(delta)
		State.FALLING_INPUT: _falling_input(delta)
		State.DEAD:          _dead(delta)

func _idle(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2.ZERO
	move_and_slide()
	if not is_on_floor():
		# Floor disappeared (e.g., destructible later) — fall.
		state = State.FALLING
		return

	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		rise_target_y = position.y - TILE_SIZE
		state = State.RISING
	elif Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		rise_target_y = position.y - TILE_SIZE
		state = State.RISING
	elif Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
	elif Input.is_action_just_pressed("jump"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -jump_initial_velocity)
		state = State.JUMPING
	# Down on floor: intentionally a no-op in v1.

func _rising(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Ceiling clipped the rise. Start horizontal flight from current y.
		state = State.FLYING_H
	elif position.y <= rise_target_y:
		position.y = rise_target_y  # snap to exact tile boundary
		state = State.FLYING_H

func _flying_h(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		rebound_target_x = position.x - direction * rebound_distance
		direction = -direction
		state = State.REBOUNDING

func _flying_up(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		post_pause_state = State.FALLING_INPUT
		state = State.PAUSED

func _flying_down(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _jumping(delta: float) -> void:
	# Apply gravity; cap descent at terminal so the jump arc matches falls.
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return

	# Mid-jump arrow input cancels the arc and starts a directional launch.
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		velocity = Vector2(-flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		velocity = Vector2(flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_down"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_DOWN
		return

	# No directional input — natural arc.
	if is_on_ceiling() and velocity.y < 0:
		velocity.y = 0.0
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED
	elif is_on_floor() and velocity.y >= 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _rebounding(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	var done := false
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Hit another wall before completing the 1-tile rebound — stop here.
		done = true
	elif (direction == 1 and position.x >= rebound_target_x) or \
		 (direction == -1 and position.x <= rebound_target_x):
		position.x = rebound_target_x
		done = true
	if done:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED

func _paused(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		state = post_pause_state
		post_pause_state = State.FALLING  # reset so unset transitions default to plain falling

func _falling(delta: float) -> void:
	velocity.x = 0.0
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if is_on_floor():
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

# Fall after a fly-up ceiling bump. Same physics as _falling, but arrows are
# live: left/right relaunch horizontally, up relaunches upward, down snaps the
# fall speed straight to terminal_velocity.
func _falling_input(delta: float) -> void:
	velocity.x = 0.0
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return

	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		velocity = Vector2(-flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		velocity = Vector2(flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_down"):
		velocity.y = terminal_velocity  # snap to max fall, stay in this state

	if is_on_floor():
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _dead(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		position = spawn_position
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		direction = 0
		state = State.IDLE

func _hit_hazard() -> bool:
	for i in get_slide_collision_count():
		var col := get_slide_collision(i)
		var other := col.get_collider()
		if other != null and other.has_meta("is_hazard"):
			_die()
			return true
	return false

func _die() -> void:
	velocity = Vector2.ZERO
	pause_timer = death_pause
	state = State.DEAD

content.content:
1	
2	📄 Ricochet: Overall Design Document
3	1. Main Menu
4	The Main Menu features the game title and two primary buttons:
5	
6	Play Game: Enter the level selection or start the campaign.
7	
8	Make Levels: Enter the integrated Level Editor.
9	
10	2. Gameplay Mechanics
11	The game is a 2D platformer-style world.
12	
13	Character: The hero is a 1×1 square hitbox. 1 tile = 48×48 pixels.
14	
15	Controls: Arrow Keys (Up, Down, Left, Right) for launches; Space for jump.
16	
17	State Management: The hero can only receive inputs when stationary on the ground (or moving passively on a conveyor belt).
18	
19	Movement Rules:
20	
21	Left / Right: The hero first ascends 1 tile vertically, then moves horizontally in the chosen direction.
22	
23	Up: The hero flies directly upwards.
24	
25	Down (on the ground): no effect in v1 (reserved for future use).
26	
27	Space (Jump): The hero performs a vertical jump of 2 tiles in place. **Input is accepted during the entire jump arc** (both ascent and descent) — pressing any arrow key while airborne launches the hero in that direction from the current mid-air position. With no input, the jump rises 2 tiles, pauses, and falls naturally. Once a directional launch is triggered (from ground or mid-jump), the hero becomes non-controllable until an obstacle is hit.
28	
29	Execution: Once a directional launch (L/R/Up/Down-from-jump) starts, the hero becomes non-controllable until an obstacle is hit.
30	
31	Obstacle Interaction:
32	
33	Lethal Obstacles: Character dies immediately.
34	
35	Special Obstacles: Triggers specific abilities (e.g., Adhesion, Teleportation).
36	
37	Standard Obstacles:
38	
39	If flying horizontally or upwards: The hero hits the wall, pauses briefly, and then starts to fall. If it's a horizontal hit, the hero rebounds by 1 tile before falling.
40	
41	If moving downwards: The hero stops and lands on the obstacle.
42	
43	Recovery: Control is returned to the player once the hero has landed or rebounded.
44	
45	3. Level & Viewport Logic
46	Viewport: 25×20 tiles = 1200×960 pixels. When launched directly via "Play Game" the OS window is exactly 1200×960. When test-played from inside the editor, the same 1200×960 viewport renders inside the editor's left edit area; the editor's full window stays 1600×960.
47	
48	Pages: A level consists of multiple "Pages." Each page can have custom dimensions.
49	
50	Centering: If a Page is smaller than the viewport, it is centered with a blank fill.
51	
52	Scrolling: If a Page is larger than the viewport, the camera follows the hero. The camera stops at the map boundaries to prevent the "void" from entering the viewport.
53	
54	Core Elements:
55	
56	Walls: Standard collision blocks. Map boundaries are treated as walls by default.
57	
58	Spawn Point: One per Page.
59	
60	Exit Point: One per Level.
61	
62	Teleports: Inter-page portals. These contain a parameter defining the target Page ID.
63	
64	Data Serialization: Level data is stored in JSON format.
65	
66	Game Flow: Players start at Page 0. Reaching a teleport triggers a quick fade-to-black and transitions the player to the target Page's spawn point.
67	
68	4. Game Parameters (Default Constants)
69	Tile size: 48×48 pixels.
70	
71	Flight Speed: 5 tiles/second.
72	
73	Rebound Distance: 1 tile.
74	
75	Jump Height (Space, vertical): 2 tiles.
76	
77	Gravity (g): 10 tiles/sec².
78	
79	Terminal Velocity: Matches the flight speed (5 tiles/second).
80	
81	5. Level Editor
82	The editor provides a full-featured suite for creation and management.
83

file.content:
📄 Ricochet: Overall Design Document
1. Main Menu
The Main Menu features the game title and two primary buttons:

Play Game: Enter the level selection or start the campaign.

Make Levels: Enter the integrated Level Editor.

2. Gameplay Mechanics
The game is a 2D platformer-style world.

Character: The hero is a 1×1 square hitbox. 1 tile = 48×48 pixels.

Controls: Arrow Keys (Up, Down, Left, Right) for launches; Space for jump.

State Management: The hero can only receive inputs when stationary on the ground (or moving passively on a conveyor belt).

Movement Rules:

Left / Right: The hero first ascends 1 tile vertically, then moves horizontally in the chosen direction.

Up: The hero flies directly upwards.

Down (on the ground): no effect in v1 (reserved for future use).

Space (Jump): The hero performs a vertical jump of 2 tiles in place. **Input is accepted during the entire jump arc** (both ascent and descent) — pressing any arrow key while airborne launches the hero in that direction from the current mid-air position. With no input, the jump rises 2 tiles, pauses, and falls naturally. Once a directional launch is triggered (from ground or mid-jump), the hero becomes non-controllable until an obstacle is hit.

Execution: Once a directional launch (L/R/Up/Down-from-jump) starts, the hero becomes non-controllable until an obstacle is hit.

Obstacle Interaction:

Lethal Obstacles: Character dies immediately.

Special Obstacles: Triggers specific abilities (e.g., Adhesion, Teleportation).

Standard Obstacles:

If flying horizontally or upwards: The hero hits the wall, pauses briefly, and then starts to fall. If it's a horizontal hit, the hero rebounds by 1 tile before falling.

If moving downwards: The hero stops and lands on the obstacle.

Recovery: Control is returned to the player once the hero has landed or rebounded.

3. Level & Viewport Logic
Viewport: 25×20 tiles = 1200×960 pixels. When launched directly via "Play Game" the OS window is exactly 1200×960. When test-played from inside the editor, the same 1200×960 viewport renders inside the editor's left edit area; the editor's full window stays 1600×960.

Pages: A level consists of multiple "Pages." Each page can have custom dimensions.

Centering: If a Page is smaller than the viewport, it is centered with a blank fill.

Scrolling: If a Page is larger than the viewport, the camera follows the hero. The camera stops at the map boundaries to prevent the "void" from entering the viewport.

Core Elements:

Walls: Standard collision blocks. Map boundaries are treated as walls by default.

Spawn Point: One per Page.

Exit Point: One per Level.

Teleports: Inter-page portals. These contain a parameter defining the target Page ID.

Data Serialization: Level data is stored in JSON format.

Game Flow: Players start at Page 0. Reaching a teleport triggers a quick fade-to-black and transitions the player to the target Page's spawn point.

4. Game Parameters (Default Constants)
Tile size: 48×48 pixels.

Flight Speed: 5 tiles/second.

Rebound Distance: 1 tile.

Jump Height (Space, vertical): 2 tiles.

Gravity (g): 10 tiles/sec².

Terminal Velocity: Matches the flight speed (5 tiles/second).

5. Level Editor
The editor provides a full-featured suite for creation and management.

Level Management: List, Create, and Delete levels.

New levels require a name and are assigned a unique ID. **The ID is stored as a string in the level data file.** Locally generated IDs are 6-digit numeric strings starting at "100000"; once the game is hosted online, IDs will be alphanumeric short strings (e.g. "aB3xK9") issued by the backend. The game and editor treat the ID as an opaque string and do not assume any particular format.

UI Layout: 1600x960 total resolution.

Edit Area: 1200x960 (Left).

Toolbar: 400x960 (Right).

Canvas Logic:

Edit area: 25×20 tiles at 48 px/tile = 1200×960 pixels (matches the in-game viewport exactly). Maximum Page size: 64×64 tiles.

Auto-Sizing: The editor automatically calculates the final Page dimensions based on the furthest placed elements upon saving.

Navigation: Right-click to drag the map. Double-click (Left) to toggle between "Fit-to-Screen" (View Only) and "1:1 Zoom" (Edit Mode).

Toolbar Controls:

Page Navigation: Prev/Next page (with auto-save).

Page Management: Create new (highest index) or Delete current (with confirmation).

Clean-up Logic: Deleting a page automatically removes any teleportation points pointing to it and re-indexes subsequent pages.

Playtest Mode: Toggle to instantly test the current page.

Element Palette: Status-based icons for Walls, Teleports (requires target input), Spawn Points (max 1 per page), and Exit Points (max 1 per level).

content.text:
Yes — re-read `games/ricochet/design/design.md`. Quick summary of what the editor needs to ship:

**Main menu:** title + Play / Make Levels buttons.

**Editor UI:** 1600×960 — left 1200×960 canvas (25×20 tiles, max 64×64 per page), right 400×960 toolbar. Right-click pan, double-click toggle fit-vs-1:1.

**Per-level model:** name + opaque string ID (local: 6-digit numeric starting "100000"), 1+ pages, **one Exit per level**, **one Spawn per page**. Auto-size pages from furthest placed element on save.

**Element palette (v1):** Walls, Teleports (require target Page ID), Spawn, Exit.

**Toolbar:** Prev/Next page (auto-save on switch), Create page (highest index), Delete page (confirm + cascade-clean teleports pointing at it + re-index later pages), Playtest current page.

**Storage:** JSON. Multi-page levels. Player starts on Page 0, teleports trigger fade + transition.

---

Before writing any editor code, four things I want to confirm — the answers shape the entire architecture:

1. **Storage location.** `user://levels/` (writable, per-install, where user content belongs) for created levels, plus optionally bundled defaults shipped in `res://levels/`? Or somewhere else for now?

2. **Hazards in the palette.** Design §2 lists "Lethal Obstacles" but §5's element palette only names Walls / Teleports / Spawn / Exit. The current `play.gd` test room has hazards (`X`). Add Hazard to the palette in v1, or defer?

3. **Build order — schema-first.** I'd strongly recommend building the **JSON schema + level loader first** (replacing the hardcoded room in `play.gd`), then building the editor on top of it. That way the runtime side is locked down before the editor exists, and you can hand-edit a JSON to test the loader without an editor. Sound good?

4. **Special tiles from §2** (Adhesion, Teleportation outside of inter-page portals, etc.) — these are mentioned but not detailed. Skip for v1, palette-only-what's-spec'd?

Answer these and I'll lay out a phased plan.

content.text:
**Phased plan:**

| # | Phase | What lands |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Schema + loader** | Define JSON shape, write level loader, replace `play.gd`'s hardcoded `TEST_ROOM` with a JSON-loaded single-page level. Hand-author 1-2 test JSONs to validate. |
| 2 | **Multi-page + teleports** | Loader handles N pages, teleport portals trigger fade + page swap + spawn at target. Exit reaching = level-complete signal. |
| 3 | **Main menu + level select** | Title screen with Play / Make. Play opens a list of bundled `res://levels/` plus user `user://levels/`. |
| 4 | **Editor skeleton** | 1600×960 layout (1200 canvas + 400 toolbar). Grid rendering, right-click pan, double-click fit/1:1 toggle. No editing yet. |
| 5 | **Editor placement** | Palette (Walls / Teleports / Spawn / Exit), click-place, enforce 1 Spawn/page and 1 Exit/level. Teleport target-page input. |
| 6 | **Editor pages + save** | Prev/Next/Create/Delete page, auto-save on switch, auto-size on save, cascade-clean teleports when a page is deleted. |
| 7 | **Editor level list** | List/Create/Delete levels, ID generation starting at "100000". |
| 8 | **Editor playtest** | Toggle to play current page, restore editor on exit. |

---

**Two design questions for Phase 1 specifically:**

**A. JSON shape — tile-grid or object-list?**

*Tile grid* (like `play.gd:11`'s string array): compact, human-readable, easy to hand-author. Walls are just `W` characters.
```json
{ "id": "100000", "name": "first",
  "pages": [{ "tiles": ["WWWWW","W.S.W","WWWWW"],
              "teleports": [{"x":3,"y":1,"target_page":1}] }] }
```

*Object list*: every element is `{type, x, y, ...}`. Flexible, editor-friendly, but verbose and unreadable by hand.

**Hybrid (my recommendation):** tile grid for cheap static stuff (walls + future hazards as different chars), separate object lists for things with parameters (teleports with targets) or singletons (`spawn`, `exit`). Best of both — hand-authorable and the editor still has structure for parameterized elements.

**B. Where does Phase 1 stop loading from?**
For Phase 1 the easiest test is a hardcoded path — `res://levels/test.json` — wired straight into `play.gd`. The level-select UI comes in Phase 3. Sound good?

Once you answer A and B, I'll write Phase 1.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
extends Node2D

# Phase 1 play scene: builds a hardcoded 25x20 test room, drops a Player at
# the spawn marker, and renders walls/hazards as colored rectangles. No
# camera, no level loader — the room exactly matches the 1200x960 viewport.

const TILE_SIZE := 48.0

# Test room. 25 cols × 20 rows.
#   W = wall   X = hazard   S = spawn   . = empty
const TEST_ROOM := [
	"WWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWW",  #  0
	"W.......................W",  #  1
	"W.......................W",  #  2
	"W.......................W",  #  3
	"W.......................W",  #  4
	"W.......................W",  #  5
	"W.......................W",  #  6
	"W.WWWW...........WWWW...W",  #  7  side platforms
	"W.......................W",  #  8
	"W...........X...........W",  #  9  hazard directly above spawn — press Up to test death
	"W.......................W",  # 10
	"W.......................W",  # 11
	"W..WWW............WWW...W",  # 12  side platforms
	"W.......................W",  # 13
	"W.......................W",  # 14
	"W.......................W",  # 15
	"W.......................W",  # 16
	"W.......................W",  # 17
	"W...........S...........W",  # 18  spawn
	"WWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWW",  # 19
]

const COLOR_WALL := Color(0.45, 0.46, 0.50, 1.0)
const COLOR_HAZARD := Color(0.85, 0.25, 0.25, 1.0)
const COLOR_PLAYER := Color(0.30, 0.65, 1.00, 1.0)
const COLOR_BG := Color(0.13, 0.14, 0.17, 1.0)

func _ready() -> void:
	_paint_background()
	var spawn := _build_room()
	_spawn_player(spawn)

func _paint_background() -> void:
	var bg := ColorRect.new()
	bg.color = COLOR_BG
	bg.position = Vector2.ZERO
	bg.size = Vector2(25 * TILE_SIZE, 20 * TILE_SIZE)
	bg.z_index = -10
	add_child(bg)

func _build_room() -> Vector2:
	var spawn := Vector2.ZERO
	for r in TEST_ROOM.size():
		var row: String = TEST_ROOM[r]
		for c in row.length():
			var ch: String = row.substr(c, 1)
			match ch:
				"W":
					_make_solid(c, r, COLOR_WALL, false)
				"X":
					_make_solid(c, r, COLOR_HAZARD, true)
				"S":
					spawn = _cell_center(c, r)
	if spawn == Vector2.ZERO:
		push_warning("[play] no spawn 'S' in test room — defaulting to (1,1)")
		spawn = _cell_center(1, 1)
	return spawn

func _make_solid(col: int, row: int, color: Color, is_hazard: bool) -> void:
	var body := StaticBody2D.new()
	body.position = _cell_center(col, row)
	body.collision_layer = 1
	if is_hazard:
		body.set_meta("is_hazard", true)

	var shape := CollisionShape2D.new()
	var rect := RectangleShape2D.new()
	rect.size = Vector2(TILE_SIZE, TILE_SIZE)
	shape.shape = rect
	body.add_child(shape)

	var visual := ColorRect.new()
	visual.position = Vector2(-TILE_SIZE / 2.0, -TILE_SIZE / 2.0)
	visual.size = Vector2(TILE_SIZE, TILE_SIZE)
	visual.color = color
	body.add_child(visual)

	add_child(body)

func _spawn_player(at: Vector2) -> void:
	var player := Player.new()
	player.position = at
	player.spawn_position = at
	player.collision_layer = 2
	player.collision_mask = 1

	var shape := CollisionShape2D.new()
	var rect := RectangleShape2D.new()
	# Slightly inset so we don't snag corners on tile-aligned walls.
	rect.size = Vector2(TILE_SIZE - 2.0, TILE_SIZE - 2.0)
	shape.shape = rect
	player.add_child(shape)

	var visual := ColorRect.new()
	visual.position = Vector2(-TILE_SIZE / 2.0, -TILE_SIZE / 2.0)
	visual.size = Vector2(TILE_SIZE, TILE_SIZE)
	visual.color = COLOR_PLAYER
	player.add_child(visual)

	add_child(player)

func _cell_center(col: int, row: int) -> Vector2:
	return Vector2(col * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2.0, row * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2.0)

content.text:
That's the right call. The editor IS the dev tool for everything that comes after, so building it next gives every later element a tight build → place → playtest loop. Reordering the phases:

| # | New order | What lands |
|---|---|---|
| ✅ 1 | Schema + loader (DONE) | |
| 2 | **Editor skeleton** | 1600×960 layout, canvas grid, right-click pan, double-click fit/1:1 toggle. No tools yet. |
| 3 | **Single-page placement** | Palette: Walls, Spawn (1 max), Exit (1 max). Click-place / right-click-erase. Defer Teleports until multi-page lands. |
| 4 | **Save / load** | Editor reads + writes the same JSON the loader uses. Round-trip works. |
| 5 | **Playtest toggle** | Editor saves, swaps to play scene, Esc returns to editor. |
| — | (now you can dev new elements with full feedback loop) | |
| 6 | Multi-page + teleports + exit functional | Loader + editor page management together. |
| 7 | Main menu | Title + Play / Make. |
| 8 | Level list | List/Create/Delete, ID generation. |

---

**Two questions before I start Phase 2 (editor skeleton):**

**A. Boot scene during dev.** Right now `project.godot:14` boots into `res://scenes/play/play.tscn`. While we're building the editor, easiest is to change the main scene to the editor scene so F5 opens the editor directly. Playtest swaps to play, Esc swaps back. OK to change `project.godot`?

**B. Editor's working file path.** The level the editor edits / saves / playtests has to live somewhere writable. Options:
- `user://levels/dev.json` — writable in both editor and exported builds. Cleanest. Survives across runs. Won't be in your repo (lives in Godot's per-user data dir).
- `res://levels/test.json` — same path the loader currently uses. Writable while running from the Godot editor on your machine, breaks in exported builds. Lives in the repo so changes are git-trackable.

The repo-tracked option is more useful right now (you can diff your test levels in git, share them with me through the file system). I'd recommend **`res://levels/test.json`** for the dev phase, then switch to `user://` once the level list lands and we have proper user-content separation.

Pick A (yes/no) and B (which path), then I'll start the editor skeleton.

content.text:
Phase 3 done. F5 should show:
- 4 tool buttons in the right toolbar: **Wall / Spawn / Exit / Erase**. Wall is selected on launch.
- **Left-click** in the edit area places the selected tool at that tile.
- Singleton enforcement: placing **Spawn** moves the existing spawn marker; placing **Exit** moves the existing exit. Placing a Wall or Erase on the spawn/exit cell removes the marker (but doesn't auto-relocate it — you re-place it manually).

**Behavior notes:**
- Edits are in-memory only — restart wipes them. Save lands in Phase 4.
- A side-effect of double-click-zoom: the first click of a double-click also fires as a single click, so you may briefly place a tile before the zoom toggles. Easy to clean up with Erase. We can move zoom to a toolbar button later if it's annoying.

Want Phase 4 (save → JSON, with the editor reading + writing `res://levels/test.json`) next?

content.text:
Phase 5 done. Dev loop is complete:

1. F5 → editor opens with `test.json` loaded.
2. Edit, click **Save** (or skip — Playtest auto-saves first).
3. Click **Playtest** → swaps to play scene running your level.
4. Press **Esc** → returns to editor.

**Cosmetic deviation from spec:** the design doc says playtest renders inside the editor's left 1200×960 area while the toolbar stays visible. I implemented a full scene-swap instead — simpler, still gets you the dev loop. Side-effect: the OS window stays at 1600×960 during play, so the play view (1200×960) sits in the top-left and the right 400×960 stays the project's default clear color. We can switch to embedded playtest later when it bothers you.

Try it: open the editor, place a few walls, hit Playtest, run around, Esc back. The wall changes you saved should be there both in the play scene AND when you return to the editor (which reloads from disk on entry).

input.new_string:
func _build_borders(width: int, height: int) -> void:
	# Invisible walls sealing the page perimeter, so a missing edge tile
	# doesn't let the player escape into the void. Same collision_layer as
	# real walls (1) so the player rebounds the same way.
	var w_px := width * TILE_SIZE
	var h_px := height * TILE_SIZE
	var t := TILE_SIZE  # thickness; matches a tile so corners overlap cleanly
	_make_border(Vector2(w_px / 2.0, -t / 2.0), Vector2(w_px + 2.0 * t, t))         # top
	_make_border(Vector2(w_px / 2.0, h_px + t / 2.0), Vector2(w_px + 2.0 * t, t))    # bottom
	_make_border(Vector2(-t / 2.0, h_px / 2.0), Vector2(t, h_px + 2.0 * t))          # left
	_make_border(Vector2(w_px + t / 2.0, h_px / 2.0), Vector2(t, h_px + 2.0 * t))    # right

func _make_border(at: Vector2, size: Vector2) -> void:
	var body := StaticBody2D.new()
	body.position = at
	body.collision_layer = 1
	var shape := CollisionShape2D.new()
	var rect := RectangleShape2D.new()
	rect.size = size
	shape.shape = rect
	body.add_child(shape)
	add_child(body)

func _build_exit(level: Dictionary, current_page: int) -> void:

message.content: When I drag the map by right mouse button, it works. But I cannot put element outside the original border of map. the size of map should not limit to put new element. final size of map should be calculated when save or playtest.

content.text:
Two changes needed in `editor.gd`:

1. **Allow placement outside current page** — grow the tile array (left/right/top/bottom) on click so any cell becomes valid; preserve visuals during left/top growth by translating `page_root` to compensate.
2. **Auto-size on save** — normalize the page to the bounding box of actual placements; preserve visuals across the trim by translating `page_root` again.

Eraser stays bounds-checked (no growth — clicking far outside doesn't extend the page just to "erase" empty space).

input.old_string:
func _apply_tool_at(col: int, row: int) -> void:
	if level_data.is_empty():
		return
	var page: Dictionary = level_data.pages[0]
	var tiles: Array = page.tiles
	if row < 0 or row >= tiles.size():
		return
	if col < 0 or col >= (tiles[0] as String).length():
		return

	match current_tool:
		Tool.WALL:
			_set_tile_char(col, row, "W")
			_clear_spawn_at(col, row)
			_clear_exit_at(col, row)
		Tool.ERASER:
			_set_tile_char(col, row, ".")
			_clear_spawn_at(col, row)
			_clear_exit_at(col, row)
		Tool.SPAWN:
			_set_tile_char(col, row, ".")
			page.spawn = {"x": col, "y": row}
			_clear_exit_at(col, row)
		Tool.EXIT:
			_set_tile_char(col, row, ".")
			level_data.exit = {"page": 0, "x": col, "y": row}
			_clear_spawn_at(col, row)
	_rebuild_page_visuals()

content.text:
Done. Behavior:

**Placement (Wall / Spawn / Exit):** click anywhere — including outside the current page bg or at panned-far positions. The page grows in that direction; existing element coords get shifted if growth was on the left/top, and `page_root` translates so what's already on screen stays put. Erase is unchanged (only acts on existing cells).

**Save:** computes the bounding box of all placements (walls + spawn + exit + teleports) and trims/shifts the page to fit. Empty rows/columns at any edge are dropped. The visual on screen stays where it was.

**Try it:** in the editor, pan right, click somewhere far past the current right edge — a wall appears, page extends. Erase a column from the existing left wall, click Save — the page bbox shrinks and the saved JSON is correspondingly compact. Reopen the JSON in a text editor to confirm.

message.content: Currently, the collision boxes strictly adhere to the grid dimensions; consequently, when moving along a wall, upward movement is obstructed by the wall (subject to a potential margin of error of approximately 1 pixel). Would it be possible to implement a mechanism where the collision box is horizontally reduced by 2 pixels during vertical movement, and vertically reduced by 2 pixels during horizontal movement, in order to prevent this type of obstruction?

content.text: Right — that's the classic "corner snag" problem. The fix is to dynamically narrow the collision rect along the perpendicular axis based on current motion. Modifying `player.gd`.

input.old_string: const TILE_SIZE := 48.0

const TILE_SIZE := 48.0

# Collision-rect sizes. Narrowed by 2px on the perpendicular axis during
# motion so the box doesn't snag on the corner of an adjacent wall while
# sliding along it.
const _SHAPE_FULL := Vector2(TILE_SIZE - 2.0, TILE_SIZE - 2.0)   # 46 × 46
const _SHAPE_HMOVE := Vector2(TILE_SIZE - 2.0, TILE_SIZE - 4.0)  # 46 × 44 — moving horizontally
const _SHAPE_VMOVE := Vector2(TILE_SIZE - 4.0, TILE_SIZE - 2.0)  # 44 × 46 — moving vertically

class_name Player
extends CharacterBody2D

# Tile-based launch-and-stop player for Ricochet.
# State machine, no smooth easing — constant flight speed, gravity for falls
# and the Space jump arc. See games/ricochet/design/design.md for the spec.

const TILE_SIZE := 48.0

# Tuning — exposed for tweaking in the Godot Inspector once Player becomes a
# scene. Speeds/distances are in tiles (multiplied by TILE_SIZE in _ready).
@export_group("Tuning")
@export var flight_speed_tiles: float = 25.0       # all directed launches
@export var gravity_tiles: float = 10.0            # tiles / sec²
@export var terminal_velocity_tiles: float = 5.0   # fall speed cap
@export var jump_height_tiles: float = 2.0         # Space-jump peak above floor
@export var rebound_distance_tiles: float = 1.0
@export var pause_time: float = 0.1                # seconds at apex / after rebound, before falling
@export var death_pause: float = 0.5               # seconds dead before respawn

# Cached pixel-space values derived from the exports above (set in _ready).
var flight_speed: float
var gravity: float
var terminal_velocity: float
var jump_height: float
var rebound_distance: float
# Initial up-velocity to reach jump_height under gravity: v = sqrt(2*g*h).
var jump_initial_velocity: float

enum State {
	IDLE,           # standing on a floor, accepting input
	RISING,         # rising 1 tile vertically before a horizontal launch
	FLYING_H,       # cruising left or right at flight speed
	FLYING_UP,      # cruising up at flight speed
	FLYING_DOWN,    # cruising down at flight speed (only triggered mid-jump)
	JUMPING,        # vertical jump arc; input accepted during ascent AND descent
	REBOUNDING,     # 1-tile horizontal rebound after hitting a wall
	PAUSED,         # brief delay before falling under gravity
	FALLING,        # gravity-driven free fall, no input
	FALLING_INPUT,  # gravity-driven fall after a fly-up ceiling bump; input accepted
	DEAD,           # waiting to respawn
}

var state: State = State.IDLE
var direction: int = 0           # -1 left / +1 right while horizontal-launched
var rise_target_y: float = 0.0   # y-coord to stop the pre-launch rise at
var rebound_target_x: float = 0.0
var pause_timer: float = 0.0
var post_pause_state: State = State.FALLING  # state to enter when PAUSED ends
var spawn_position: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO

const TUNING_PATH := "res://config/player_tuning.json"

func _ready() -> void:
	_load_tuning(TUNING_PATH)
	flight_speed = flight_speed_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	gravity = gravity_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	terminal_velocity = terminal_velocity_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	jump_height = jump_height_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	rebound_distance = rebound_distance_tiles * TILE_SIZE
	jump_initial_velocity = sqrt(2.0 * gravity * jump_height)

	# Register the Space jump action if the project doesn't already define it.
	if not InputMap.has_action("jump"):
		InputMap.add_action("jump")
		var ev := InputEventKey.new()
		ev.physical_keycode = KEY_SPACE
		InputMap.action_add_event("jump", ev)

# Override @export defaults with values from a JSON file. Any keys present in
# the JSON win; missing keys / missing file / parse errors fall back to defaults.
func _load_tuning(path: String) -> void:
	if not FileAccess.file_exists(path):
		return
	var f := FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.READ)
	if f == null:
		push_warning("[player] could not open tuning file: %s" % path)
		return
	var data: Variant = JSON.parse_string(f.get_as_text())
	if typeof(data) != TYPE_DICTIONARY:
		push_warning("[player] tuning json is not an object: %s" % path)
		return
	for key in data:
		if key in self:
			set(key, data[key])
		else:
			push_warning("[player] unknown tuning key '%s' in %s" % [key, path])

func _physics_process(delta: float) -> void:
	match state:
		State.IDLE:        _idle(delta)
		State.RISING:      _rising(delta)
		State.FLYING_H:    _flying_h(delta)
		State.FLYING_UP:   _flying_up(delta)
		State.FLYING_DOWN: _flying_down(delta)
		State.JUMPING:     _jumping(delta)
		State.REBOUNDING:  _rebounding(delta)
		State.PAUSED:        _paused(delta)
		State.FALLING:       _falling(delta)
		State.FALLING_INPUT: _falling_input(delta)
		State.DEAD:          _dead(delta)

func _idle(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2.ZERO
	move_and_slide()
	if not is_on_floor():
		# Floor disappeared (e.g., destructible later) — fall.
		state = State.FALLING
		return

	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		rise_target_y = position.y - TILE_SIZE
		state = State.RISING
	elif Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		rise_target_y = position.y - TILE_SIZE
		state = State.RISING
	elif Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
	elif Input.is_action_just_pressed("jump"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -jump_initial_velocity)
		state = State.JUMPING
	# Down on floor: intentionally a no-op in v1.

func _rising(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Ceiling clipped the rise. Start horizontal flight from current y.
		state = State.FLYING_H
	elif position.y <= rise_target_y:
		position.y = rise_target_y  # snap to exact tile boundary
		state = State.FLYING_H

func _flying_h(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		rebound_target_x = position.x - direction * rebound_distance
		direction = -direction
		state = State.REBOUNDING

func _flying_up(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		post_pause_state = State.FALLING_INPUT
		state = State.PAUSED

func _flying_down(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _jumping(delta: float) -> void:
	# Apply gravity; cap descent at terminal so the jump arc matches falls.
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return

	# Mid-jump arrow input cancels the arc and starts a directional launch.
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		velocity = Vector2(-flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		velocity = Vector2(flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_down"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_DOWN
		return

	# No directional input — natural arc.
	if is_on_ceiling() and velocity.y < 0:
		velocity.y = 0.0
		pause_timer = pause_time
		post_pause_state = State.FALLING_INPUT
		state = State.PAUSED
	elif is_on_floor() and velocity.y >= 0:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _rebounding(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	var done := false
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Hit another wall before completing the 1-tile rebound — stop here.
		done = true
	elif (direction == 1 and position.x >= rebound_target_x) or \
		 (direction == -1 and position.x <= rebound_target_x):
		position.x = rebound_target_x
		done = true
	if done:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		state = State.PAUSED

func _paused(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		state = post_pause_state
		post_pause_state = State.FALLING  # reset so unset transitions default to plain falling

func _falling(delta: float) -> void:
	velocity.x = 0.0
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	if is_on_floor():
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

# Fall after a fly-up ceiling bump. Same physics as _falling, but arrows are
# live: left/right relaunch horizontally, up relaunches upward, down snaps the
# fall speed straight to terminal_velocity.
func _falling_input(delta: float) -> void:
	velocity.x = 0.0
	velocity.y += gravity * delta
	velocity.y = minf(velocity.y, terminal_velocity)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return

	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		velocity = Vector2(-flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		velocity = Vector2(flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_down"):
		velocity.y = terminal_velocity  # snap to max fall, stay in this state

	if is_on_floor():
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		state = State.IDLE

func _dead(delta: float) -> void:
	pause_timer -= delta
	if pause_timer <= 0.0:
		position = spawn_position
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		direction = 0
		state = State.IDLE

func _hit_hazard() -> bool:
	for i in get_slide_collision_count():
		var col := get_slide_collision(i)
		var other := col.get_collider()
		if other != null and other.has_meta("is_hazard"):
			_die()
			return true
	return false

func _die() -> void:
	velocity = Vector2.ZERO
	pause_timer = death_pause
	state = State.DEAD

content.text:
Done. Behavior:

- **Idle / paused / dead**: full 46×46 collision rect (unchanged baseline).
- **Horizontal motion** (`FLYING_H`, `REBOUNDING`): 46×44 — top and bottom inset 1px each, so a flat ceiling or floor doesn't snag the corner.
- **Vertical motion** (`RISING`, `FLYING_UP`, `FLYING_DOWN`, `JUMPING`, `FALLING`, `FALLING_INPUT`): 44×46 — left and right inset 1px each, so a wall flush against the player's path doesn't snag.

The shape is checked once per physics frame at the top of `_physics_process` (`player.gd:_update_collision_shape`), so transitions take effect on the next frame. The visual `ColorRect` is unchanged at 48×48 — only the collision rect resizes.

Test by sliding the player against a wall while pressing Up — should now climb cleanly without the 1px hitch.

content.text:
Done. Sequence is now: horizontal flight → wall → 1-tile rebound → brief pause → **FALLING_INPUT** (gravity + arrows live + down-snap to terminal). Rebound and pause are kept (they're in the design spec), but the moment the pause ends you can launch in any direction from mid-air without waiting to land.

If you'd also like the rebound itself to accept input (interrupting the 1-tile bounce-back when an arrow is pressed), or to skip the pause entirely so input comes back even faster, let me know — those are small follow-ups.

message.content: There is no need to wait for a fall; the object can accept new commands immediately upon rebounding. This allows it to repeatedly strike the wall and bounce back—striking, rebounding, and striking again—thereby achieving a result akin to hovering in mid-air.

content.text: Adding mid-rebound input handlers to `_rebounding` so any arrow-key press during the bounce-back interrupts it and launches in that direction. Same pattern as `_jumping` and `_falling_input`.

input.old_string:
func _rebounding(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return
	var done := false
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Hit another wall before completing the 1-tile rebound — stop here.
		done = true
	elif (direction == 1 and position.x >= rebound_target_x) or \
		 (direction == -1 and position.x <= rebound_target_x):
		position.x = rebound_target_x
		done = true
	if done:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		post_pause_state = State.FALLING_INPUT
		state = State.PAUSED
input.new_string:
func _rebounding(_delta: float) -> void:
	velocity = Vector2(direction * flight_speed, 0.0)
	move_and_slide()
	if _hit_hazard():
		return

	# Mid-rebound arrow input cancels the 1-tile bounce-back and launches
	# in the new direction. Pressing toward the wall just hit produces a
	# bounce-bounce hover loop, which is intentional.
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_left"):
		direction = -1
		velocity = Vector2(-flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_right"):
		direction = 1
		velocity = Vector2(flight_speed, 0.0)
		state = State.FLYING_H
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_up"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, -flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_UP
		return
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_down"):
		velocity = Vector2(0.0, flight_speed)
		state = State.FLYING_DOWN
		return

	var done := false
	if get_slide_collision_count() > 0:
		# Hit another wall before completing the 1-tile rebound — stop here.
		done = true
	elif (direction == 1 and position.x >= rebound_target_x) or \
		 (direction == -1 and position.x <= rebound_target_x):
		position.x = rebound_target_x
		done = true
	if done:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		pause_timer = pause_time
		post_pause_state = State.FALLING_INPUT
		state = State.PAUSED

During a horizontal rebound, any arrow press interrupts the bounce and launches in that direction — including pressing the same direction as the original flight (which produces the hit-rebound-hit-rebound hover). If the rebound completes without interruption, the existing pause-then-`FALLING_INPUT` path still gives you input mid-fall.

content.content:
1	extends Node2D
2	
3	# Phase 3 editor: 1600x960 layout with a tool palette in the right toolbar
4	# and click-to-place editing on the left canvas. Edits are in-memory only —
5	# save/load lands in Phase 4. Right-click drag pans, left-double-click toggles
6	# 1:1 / fit zoom.
7	
8	const TILE_SIZE := 48.0
9	const EDIT_W := 1200.0
10	const EDIT_H := 960.0
11	const TOOLBAR_W := 400.0
12	const TOTAL_W := EDIT_W + TOOLBAR_W
13	const TOTAL_H := EDIT_H
14	
15	const TEST_LEVEL_PATH := "res://levels/test.json"
16	
17	const COLOR_EDITOR_BG := Color(0.10, 0.11, 0.13, 1.0)
18	const COLOR_PAGE_BG := Color(0.16, 0.17, 0.20, 1.0)
19	const COLOR_GRID := Color(0.30, 0.32, 0.36, 0.6)
20	const COLOR_WALL := Color(0.45, 0.46, 0.50, 1.0)
21	const COLOR_SPAWN := Color(0.30, 0.65, 1.00, 1.0)
22	const COLOR_EXIT := Color(0.40, 0.85, 0.45, 1.0)
23	const COLOR_TOOLBAR_BG := Color(0.20, 0.21, 0.24, 1.0)
24	
25	enum Zoom { ONE_TO_ONE, FIT }
26	enum Tool { WALL, SPAWN, EXIT, ERASER }
27	
28	const TOOL_LABELS := {
29		Tool.WALL: "Wall",
30		Tool.SPAWN: "Spawn",
31		Tool.EXIT: "Exit",
32		Tool.ERASER: "Erase",
33	}
34	
35	var page_root: Node2D
36	var pan_active := false
37	var pan_anchor_screen: Vector2
38	var pan_anchor_root: Vector2
39	var zoom_mode: Zoom = Zoom.ONE_TO_ONE
40	var fit_scale: float = 1.0
41	var page_size_px: Vector2 = Vector2(EDIT_W, EDIT_H)
42	var level_data: Dictionary = {}
43	var current_tool: Tool = Tool.WALL
44	var status_label: Label
45	
46	func _ready() -> void:
47		level_data = LevelLoader.load_level(TEST_LEVEL_PATH)
48		_build_chrome()
49		if level_data.is_empty():
50			return
51		_build_page(level_data.pages[0])
52		_apply_zoom()
53	
54	func _build_chrome() -> void:
55		var bg := ColorRect.new()
56		bg.color = COLOR_EDITOR_BG
57		bg.position = Vector2.ZERO
58		bg.size = Vector2(TOTAL_W, TOTAL_H)
59		bg.z_index = -100
60		bg.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
61		add_child(bg)
62	
63		page_root = Node2D.new()
64		add_child(page_root)
65	
66		var tb := ColorRect.new()
67		tb.color = COLOR_TOOLBAR_BG
68		tb.position = Vector2(EDIT_W, 0.0)
69		tb.size = Vector2(TOOLBAR_W, TOTAL_H)
70		tb.z_index = 100
71		tb.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
72		add_child(tb)
73	
74		var header := Label.new()
75		header.text = "TOOLS"
76		header.position = Vector2(EDIT_W + 16.0, 16.0)
77		header.z_index = 101
78		header.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
79		add_child(header)
80	
81		# Tool buttons as a radio group.
82		var group := ButtonGroup.new()
83		var y := 56.0
84		for tool_kind in [Tool.WALL, Tool.SPAWN, Tool.EXIT, Tool.ERASER]:
85			var btn := Button.new()
86			btn.text = TOOL_LABELS[tool_kind]
87			btn.position = Vector2(EDIT_W + 16.0, y)
88			btn.size = Vector2(TOOLBAR_W - 32.0, 48.0)
89			btn.toggle_mode = true
90			btn.button_group = group
91			btn.button_pressed = (tool_kind == current_tool)
92			btn.z_index = 102
93			btn.toggled.connect(_on_tool_toggled.bind(tool_kind))
94			add_child(btn)
95			y += 56.0
96	
97		# Save + Playtest buttons, then status label.
98		y += 16.0
99		var save_btn := Button.new()
100		save_btn.text = "Save"
101		save_btn.position = Vector2(EDIT_W + 16.0, y)
102		save_btn.size = Vector2(TOOLBAR_W - 32.0, 48.0)
103		save_btn.z_index = 102
104		save_btn.pressed.connect(_save_level)
105		add_child(save_btn)
106		y += 56.0
107	
108		var play_btn := Button.new()
109		play_btn.text = "Playtest"
110		play_btn.position = Vector2(EDIT_W + 16.0, y)
111		play_btn.size = Vector2(TOOLBAR_W - 32.0, 48.0)
112		play_btn.z_index = 102
113		play_btn.pressed.connect(_playtest)
114		add_child(play_btn)
115		y += 56.0
116	
117		status_label = Label.new()
118		status_label.position = Vector2(EDIT_W + 16.0, y)
119		status_label.size = Vector2(TOOLBAR_W - 32.0, 60.0)
120		status_label.autowrap_mode = TextServer.AUTOWRAP_WORD_SMART
121		status_label.z_index = 102
122		status_label.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
123		add_child(status_label)
124	
125	func _build_page(page: Dictionary) -> void:
126		var tiles: Array = page.tiles
127		var cols: int = (tiles[0] as String).length()
128		var rows: int = tiles.size()
129		page_size_px = Vector2(cols * TILE_SIZE, rows * TILE_SIZE)
130		fit_scale = minf(EDIT_W / page_size_px.x, EDIT_H / page_size_px.y)
131	
132		var page_bg := ColorRect.new()
133		page_bg.color = COLOR_PAGE_BG
134		page_bg.position = Vector2.ZERO
135		page_bg.size = page_size_px
136		page_bg.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
137		page_root.add_child(page_bg)
138	
139		var grid := _GridDrawer.new()
140		grid.tile_size = TILE_SIZE
141		grid.cols = cols
142		grid.rows = rows
143		grid.line_color = COLOR_GRID
144		page_root.add_child(grid)
145	
146		for r in tiles.size():
147			var row: String = tiles[r]
148			for c in row.length():
149				if row.substr(c, 1) == "W":
150					_add_tile_rect(c, r, COLOR_WALL)
151	
152		if page.has("spawn"):
153			_add_tile_rect(int(page.spawn.x), int(page.spawn.y), COLOR_SPAWN)
154	
155		if level_data.has("exit") and int(level_data.exit.page) == 0:
156			_add_tile_rect(int(level_data.exit.x), int(level_data.exit.y), COLOR_EXIT)
157	
158	func _add_tile_rect(col: int, row: int, color: Color) -> void:
159		var visual := ColorRect.new()
160		visual.position = Vector2(col * TILE_SIZE, row * TILE_SIZE)
161		visual.size = Vector2(TILE_SIZE, TILE_SIZE)
162		visual.color = color
163		visual.mouse_filter = Control.MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE
164		page_root.add_child(visual)
165	
166	func _rebuild_page_visuals() -> void:
167		for child in page_root.get_children():
168			child.queue_free()
169		_build_page(level_data.pages[0])
170	
171	func _apply_zoom() -> void:
172		var factor: float = 1.0
173		if zoom_mode == Zoom.FIT:
174			factor = fit_scale
175		page_root.scale = Vector2(factor, factor)
176		var scaled := page_size_px * factor
177		page_root.position = Vector2((EDIT_W - scaled.x) / 2.0, (EDIT_H - scaled.y) / 2.0)
178	
179	func _on_tool_toggled(pressed: bool, tool_kind: int) -> void:
180		if pressed:
181			current_tool = tool_kind
182	
183	func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
184		if event is InputEventMouseButton:
185			var mb: InputEventMouseButton = event
186			if mb.button_index == MOUSE_BUTTON_RIGHT:
187				if mb.pressed:
188					if mb.position.x >= EDIT_W:
189						return
190					pan_active = true
191					pan_anchor_screen = mb.position
192					pan_anchor_root = page_root.position
193				else:
194					pan_active = false
195			elif mb.button_index == MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT and mb.pressed:
196				if pan_active or mb.position.x >= EDIT_W:
197					return
198				if mb.double_click:
199					zoom_mode = Zoom.FIT if zoom_mode == Zoom.ONE_TO_ONE else Zoom.ONE_TO_ONE
200					_apply_zoom()
201				else:
202					var tile := _window_to_tile(mb.position)
203					_apply_tool_at(tile.x, tile.y)
204		elif event is InputEventMouseMotion and pan_active:
205			var mm: InputEventMouseMotion = event
206			page_root.position = pan_anchor_root + (mm.position - pan_anchor_screen)
207	
208	func _window_to_tile(window_pos: Vector2) -> Vector2i:
209		var local := (window_pos - page_root.position) / page_root.scale
210		return Vector2i(int(floor(local.x / TILE_SIZE)), int(floor(local.y / TILE_SIZE)))
211	
212	func _apply_tool_at(col: int, row: int) -> void:
213		if level_data.is_empty():
214			return
215	
216		if current_tool == Tool.ERASER:
217			# Erase only operates on existing cells; never grows the page.
218			var page: Dictionary = level_data.pages[0]
219			var tiles: Array = page.tiles
220			if row < 0 or row >= tiles.size():
221				return
222			if col < 0 or col >= (tiles[0] as String).length():
223				return
224			_set_tile_char(col, row, ".")
225			_clear_spawn_at(col, row)
226			_clear_exit_at(col, row)
227		else:
228			# Placement tools grow the page if the click is outside current bounds.
229			var grown := _grow_to_include(col, row)
230			col = grown.x
231			row = grown.y
232			match current_tool:
233				Tool.WALL:
234					_set_tile_char(col, row, "W")
235					_clear_spawn_at(col, row)
236					_clear_exit_at(col, row)
237				Tool.SPAWN:
238					_set_tile_char(col, row, ".")
239					level_data.pages[0].spawn = {"x": col, "y": row}
240					_clear_exit_at(col, row)
241				Tool.EXIT:
242					_set_tile_char(col, row, ".")
243					level_data.exit = {"page": 0, "x": col, "y": row}
244					_clear_spawn_at(col, row)
245		_rebuild_page_visuals()
246	
247	# Expands page 0's tile array so (col, row) is in bounds. For negative shifts
248	# (prepending cols/rows), all existing element coords are bumped by the shift
249	# and page_root is translated to keep visuals at the same screen position.
250	# Returns the (possibly shifted) (col, row) inside the now-grown array.
251	func _grow_to_include(col: int, row: int) -> Vector2i:
252		var page: Dictionary = level_data.pages[0]
253		var tiles: Array = page.tiles
254		var shift_x := 0 if col >= 0 else -col
255		var shift_y := 0 if row >= 0 else -row
256	
257		if shift_x > 0:
258			var pad := ".".repeat(shift_x)
259			for i in tiles.size():
260				tiles[i] = pad + (tiles[i] as String)
261			_offset_elements(0, shift_x, 0)
262			page_root.position.x -= float(shift_x) * TILE_SIZE * page_root.scale.x
263	
264		if shift_y > 0:
265			var w: int = (tiles[0] as String).length()
266			var blank := ".".repeat(w)
267			var new_rows: Array = []
268			for _i in shift_y:
269				new_rows.append(blank)
270			for r in tiles.size():
271				new_rows.append(tiles[r])
272			page.tiles = new_rows
273			tiles = new_rows
274			_offset_elements(0, 0, shift_y)
275			page_root.position.y -= float(shift_y) * TILE_SIZE * page_root.scale.y
276	
277		var new_col := col + shift_x
278		var new_row := row + shift_y
279		var w_after: int = (tiles[0] as String).length()
280		var h_after: int = tiles.size()
281	
282		if new_col >= w_after:
283			var pad := ".".repeat(new_col - w_after + 1)
284			for i in tiles.size():
285				tiles[i] = (tiles[i] as String) + pad
286	
287		if new_row >= h_after:
288			var w_final: int = (tiles[0] as String).length()
289			var blank := ".".repeat(w_final)
290			for _i in new_row - h_after + 1:
291				tiles.append(blank)
292	
293		return Vector2i(new_col, new_row)
294	
295	# Adds (dx, dy) to spawn / exit / teleport coords on the given page.
296	func _offset_elements(page_idx: int, dx: int, dy: int) -> void:
297		var page: Dictionary = level_data.pages[page_idx]
298		if page.has("spawn"):
299			page.spawn.x = int(page.spawn.x) + dx
300			page.spawn.y = int(page.spawn.y) + dy
301		if level_data.has("exit") and int(level_data.exit.page) == page_idx:
302			level_data.exit.x = int(level_data.exit.x) + dx
303			level_data.exit.y = int(level_data.exit.y) + dy
304		if page.has("teleports"):
305			for tp in (page.teleports as Array):
306				tp.x = int(tp.x) + dx
307				tp.y = int(tp.y) + dy
308	
309	# Trims page_idx to the bounding box of its placements (walls + spawn + exit
310	# + teleports) and shifts coords so the bbox is rooted at (0,0). Returns the
311	# (min_col, min_row) of the original bbox, so the caller can compensate the
312	# view to keep visuals stable.
313	func _normalize_page(page_idx: int) -> Vector2i:
314		var page: Dictionary = level_data.pages[page_idx]
315		var tiles: Array = page.tiles
316		var current_w: int = (tiles[0] as String).length()
317		var current_h: int = tiles.size()
318	
319		var min_col := current_w
320		var min_row := current_h
321		var max_col := -1
322		var max_row := -1
323	
324		for r in tiles.size():
325			var row_str: String = tiles[r]
326			for c in row_str.length():
327				if row_str.substr(c, 1) != ".":
328					if c < min_col: min_col = c
329					if c > max_col: max_col = c
330					if r < min_row: min_row = r
331					if r > max_row: max_row = r
332	
333		if page.has("spawn"):
334			var sx := int(page.spawn.x)
335			var sy := int(page.spawn.y)
336			if sx < min_col: min_col = sx
337			if sx > max_col: max_col = sx
338			if sy < min_row: min_row = sy
339			if sy > max_row: max_row = sy
340		if level_data.has("exit") and int(level_data.exit.page) == page_idx:
341			var ex := int(level_data.exit.x)
342			var ey := int(level_data.exit.y)
343			if ex < min_col: min_col = ex
344			if ex > max_col: max_col = ex
345			if ey < min_row: min_row = ey
346			if ey > max_row: max_row = ey
347		if page.has("teleports"):
348			for tp in (page.teleports as Array):
349				var tx := int(tp.x)
350				var ty := int(tp.y)
351				if tx < min_col: min_col = tx
352				if tx > max_col: max_col = tx
353				if ty < min_row: min_row = ty
354				if ty > max_row: max_row = ty
355	
356		if max_col < 0:
357			# No placements at all — collapse to a 1×1 empty page.
358			page.tiles = ["."]
359			return Vector2i.ZERO
360	
361		var dx := min_col
362		var dy := min_row
363		var new_w := max_col - dx + 1
364		var new_h := max_row - dy + 1
365	
366		var new_tiles: Array = []
367		for new_r in new_h:
368			var src_row: String = tiles[new_r + dy]
369			new_tiles.append(src_row.substr(dx, new_w))
370		page.tiles = new_tiles
371	
372		_offset_elements(page_idx, -dx, -dy)
373		return Vector2i(dx, dy)
374	
375	func _set_tile_char(col: int, row: int, ch: String) -> void:
376		var tiles: Array = level_data.pages[0].tiles
377		var row_str: String = tiles[row]
378		tiles[row] = row_str.substr(0, col) + ch + row_str.substr(col + 1)
379	
380	func _clear_spawn_at(col: int, row: int) -> void:
381		var page: Dictionary = level_data.pages[0]
382		if page.has("spawn") and int(page.spawn.x) == col and int(page.spawn.y) == row:
383			page.erase("spawn")
384	
385	func _clear_exit_at(col: int, row: int) -> void:
386		if level_data.has("exit") \
387				and int(level_data.exit.page) == 0 \
388				and int(level_data.exit.x) == col \
389				and int(level_data.exit.y) == row:
390			level_data.erase("exit")
391	
392	func _save_level() -> void:
393		if level_data.is_empty():
394			_show_status("Nothing to save")
395			return
396	
397		# Auto-size: trim each page to the bbox of its placements and shift
398		# coords to (0,0)-relative. Compensate page_root so visuals don't jump.
399		var shift := _normalize_page(0)
400		if shift != Vector2i.ZERO:
401			page_root.position += Vector2(
402				float(shift.x) * TILE_SIZE * page_root.scale.x,
403				float(shift.y) * TILE_SIZE * page_root.scale.y)
404		_rebuild_page_visuals()
405	
406		var f := FileAccess.open(TEST_LEVEL_PATH, FileAccess.WRITE)
407		if f == null:
408			push_error("[editor] could not open %s for write" % TEST_LEVEL_PATH)
409			_show_status("Save failed")
410			return
411		f.store_string(JSON.stringify(level_data, "  "))
412	
413		# Soft validation — save anyway, but flag what's missing for play.
414		var issues: Array = []
415		if not level_data.has("exit"):
416			issues.append("no Exit set")
417		for i in (level_data.pages as Array).size():
418			if not (level_data.pages[i] as Dictionary).has("spawn"):
419				issues.append("page %d has no Spawn" % i)
420		if issues.is_empty():
421			_show_status("Saved")
422		else:
423			for s in issues:
424				print("[editor] save warning: %s" % s)
425			_show_status("Saved (%d warning%s)" % [issues.size(), "" if issues.size() == 1 else "s"])
426	
427	func _show_status(text: String) -> void:
428		status_label.text = text
429		if text != "":
430			get_tree().create_timer(2.0).timeout.connect(_clear_status)
431	
432	func _clear_status() -> void:
433		status_label.text = ""
434	
435	func _playtest() -> void:
436		_save_level()
437		get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/play/play.tscn")
438	
439	
440	class _GridDrawer extends Node2D:
441		var tile_size: float = 48.0
442		var cols: int = 25
443		var rows: int = 20
444		var line_color: Color = Color(0.3, 0.3, 0.3, 0.5)
445	
446		func _draw() -> void:
447			var w := cols * tile_size
448			var h := rows * tile_size
449			for c in cols + 1:
450				draw_line(Vector2(c * tile_size, 0.0), Vector2(c * tile_size, h), line_color, 1.0)
451			for r in rows + 1:
452				draw_line(Vector2(0.0, r * tile_size), Vector2(w, r * tile_size), line_color, 1.0)
453
